hypothesis: If a conditional diffusion model operates in the chemically informed CIAE latent space and is conditioned on adsorbate identity, adsorption energy, and allowed element lists, then it will generate shell-wise catalytic surface compositions that closely match the distribution of real configurations (low Fréchet Distance), comply with chemical constraints (low Leakage), and exhibit high point-wise similarity to ground-truth configurations (high Cosine Similarity), because the CIAE encodes chemically meaningful latent representations and the conditioning injects adsorption-specific information into generation.
